channel.title: VS Code on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in VS Code on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed settings now support strictKnownMarketplaces in VS Code and GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description:
GitHub added enterprise-managed support for strictKnownMarketplaces in VS Code and GitHub Copilot CLI. The setting is in public preview and lets enterprises control which plugins users can install.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed settings now support bypass permission controls
item.description:
Enterprise administrators can now set disableBypassPermissionsMode to "disable" in enterprise-managed settings.json to prevent GitHub Copilot CLI and VS Code from bypassing permission controls.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: New project templates and template gallery for Azure Functions in VS Code
item.description:
The Azure Functions VS Code extension adds a redesigned Create New Project experience in public preview. It replaces the multi-step Quick Pick wizard with a searchable, filterable visual template gallery.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Code-first observability for Foundry Agents in VS Code
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry adds code-first observability for agent developers in public preview via an observe skill in the Foundry Plugin for VS Code Copilot Chat. The feature lets developers run the evaluation-driven optimization loop from within the editor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL Database provisioning in the MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now supports Azure SQL Database provisioning in public preview. It starts with the free tier and lets you create and connect to a managed cloud database directly from the editor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Copilot-assisted coding & agent skills to build, deploy, and run Azure Functions faster in VS Code, CLI, and azd
item.description:
Azure Functions public preview adds Copilot-assisted coding and agent skills for building, deploying, and running Functions apps in VS Code, the CLI, and azd. The preview aims to improve AI help quality by providing Azure Functions-specific guidance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Auto model selection now routes based on your task in VS Code
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection in VS Code now routes tasks to the best model based on utilization and model health metrics. GitHub says this is intended to improve quality, reliability, and token efficiency.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions now generally available on mobile, web, and VS Code
item.description:
Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions is now generally available on GitHub Mobile and github.com, with support also referenced for VS Code. It lets you start work in the terminal and continue managing the session from mobile or web.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description:
Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available. It adds VS Code support for Microsoft Agent Framework, with agent creation from templates or GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foundry.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Bring your own language model key in VS Code now available
item.description:
Copilot Business and Enterprise users can now use bring your own language model key (BYOK) in Visual Studio Code. This lets teams use their own API keys to access supported models in VS Code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection
item.description:
GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available to Copilot Student users through Copilot auto model selection. It is included in Auto for GitHub Copilot Chat in supported clients such as Visual Studio Code and Visual Studio.

  • Source: GitHub
